items.title: k-NN fraud detection
items.scenario: A bank represents each transaction as a numeric feature vector (amount, time-of-day, merchant category, velocity features). To flag anomalies, analysts compute L2 distances from a new transaction to historical normal transactions and label it suspicious if the k smallest distances are unusually large.
items.explanation: L2 distance in feature space approximates how atypical a transaction is relative to past behavior; the norm choice affects which deviations (large single feature vs many moderate ones) look farthest away.

items.title: Embedding search in a RAG system
items.scenario: A support chatbot embeds user questions and knowledge-base passages into 768-dimensional vectors. Retrieval returns passages whose embeddings have the highest cosine similarity to the question embedding.
items.explanation: Cosine similarity normalizes by L2 norm, so retrieval focuses on semantic direction rather than passage length or embedding magnitude—critical when document sizes vary widely.

items.title: Gradient clipping during LLM fine-tuning
items.scenario: During fine-tuning, an optimizer computes the L2 norm of the gradient vector across all parameters. If the norm exceeds a threshold (e.g. 1.0), the gradient is scaled down proportionally before the weight update.
items.explanation: Bounding the global L2 norm prevents a single minibatch from causing an oversized update, which stabilizes training on noisy or long-tail data.
